crises.title: Chennai Day Zero — Reservoirs Hit Zero

timeline.event: All 4 major reservoirs combined at 8% capacity. Normal monsoon fails.
Municipal supply reduced to every 3 days
Tanker prices spike 400%. Queue times 4-6 hours.
IT companies shut non-essential offices. Mass exodus begins.
Water tanker mafias emerge. Police deployed at water points.
Northeast monsoon partially restores supply. Gradual recovery.

what_worked: Early installation of rooftop rainwater harvesting
Grey water recycling (bath water for toilets)
Community water ATMs provided 20L/family/day at low cost
Pre-crisis storage of 2000L+ carried families 3-4 weeks
what_failed: Households with <7 days storage depleted in week 1
Open drums contaminated within 48 hours in 42°C heat
Borewell water unsafe (fluoride 3x safe limit in some areas)
Late rationing — families who waited too long faced illness

survival_strategies.key_insight: Households that pre-rationed to 25L/day before reserves hit 50% stretched supply 3x longer.

crises.title: Cape Town Day Zero — The Near Miss

timeline.event: Day Zero projected: taps off for all homes. 50L/person/day limit.
Limit reduced to 50L/day then 87L/day as conservation kicks in.
City-wide behavior change saves 200M liters/day vs pre-crisis.
Day Zero pushed back indefinitely. Rains begin partial recovery.

what_worked: Aggressive public communication — every resident knew their daily allocation
Real-time dam level tracking (public website updated daily)
Agriculture water cuts freed up residential supply
Grey water reuse for flushing toilet saved 30L/person/day
what_failed: Some borewell drilling created subsidence risks

survival_strategies.key_insight: 50L/day is enough to maintain dignity if you reuse grey water. The order matters: drink first, flush last.

crises.title: São Paulo Water Crisis — 20 Million at Risk

timeline.event: Cantareira system at 5%. Worst drought in 80 years.
Rationing: 12 hours supply, 36 hours off. No announcement.
Dead pool volume accessed — technically empty.
Rains return. Slow recovery over 2 years.

what_worked: Apartment building cisterns (legal requirement since 2009) gave 5-7 days buffer
Mobile tanker apps let residents track tanker locations
Reduced shower time from 8 min to 2 min cut 60L/person/day
what_failed: No public advance warning — utility company hid data
Irregular supply schedule created panic hoarding
Informal water market at 10x regulated price exploited poor

survival_strategies.key_insight: Irregular supply is worse than no supply. Predictable rationing allows planning. Unpredictability causes panic and hoarding.
